description: Behavioral evals for the synth-data skill. The CLI lives at skills/pocsynth/pocsynth.py. generate/test/presets are free + offline; extract/schema-infer spend Bedrock and must be cost-gated.

evals.name: Preset dataset, free and offline
evals.prompt: Give me 500 rows of fake B2B SaaS customer data as CSV.
evals.expected_output: Claude recognizes a bundled preset fits, does NOT run doctor (no AWS needed), invokes ./skills/pocsynth/pocsynth.py --json generate --preset b2b_saas --rows 500 (csv), and validates with test. Exit 0; rows.csv on disk.

deterministic.text: stdout is a valid JSON success envelope

behavioral.text: Claude did NOT run doctor or any Bedrock call for this free path
Claude treated generation as free (no cost gate / AskUserQuestion about spend)
Claude validated the data with test after generating, or offered to

evals.name: Validation failure routes on exit 7, no retry
evals.prompt: Validate ./out/rows.csv against ./out/schema.json — do the rows match the schema?
evals.expected_output: Claude invokes ./skills/pocsynth/pocsynth.py --json test --rows ./out/rows.csv --schema ./out/schema.json. If exit 7, it surfaces result.context.report.violations and does NOT retry the same command.

behavioral.text: On exit 7 Claude surfaced the violation summary (row/field/rule) rather than re-running
Claude did not treat exit 7 as a transient/retryable error

evals.name: Describe a business -> schema -> data
evals.prompt: I don't have a sample file. Generate synthetic data for a fintech company's transaction records.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs doctor if fresh env, uses schema --from-prompt to infer a schema (mentions it spends a small amount of Bedrock), reviews the schema, then generate + test (free).

behavioral.text: Claude used schema --from-prompt (not extract — there is no document)
Claude noted the from-prompt step spends Bedrock, but did not over-gate a pennies-level call
Claude generated rows for free after inferring the schema

evals.name: Real PDF -> schema -> unlimited free rows, cost-gated
evals.prompt: I have report.pdf with real customer data. Make me 10,000 synthetic rows that look like it.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs doctor (fresh env), runs estimate --for extract and surfaces the cost (AskUserQuestion if >$0.10) BEFORE extract, runs extract with the PII audit ON, infers a schema, then generate 10000 rows (free) + test. Communicates that generated data is shareable but the extract sample is not.

deterministic.text: estimate --for extract was invoked before extract

behavioral.text: Claude ran estimate and surfaced the cost before the paid extract
Claude did NOT pass --no-pii-audit on a real-customer-data document
Claude generated the 10k rows for free from the inferred schema (did not loop paid calls)
Claude communicated that the generated dataset is safe to share but the extract sample/audit CSV are not

evals.name: Does not misroute a data request to document conversion
evals.prompt: Mock me a users table with 1000 rows.
evals.expected_output: Claude uses the data pipeline (preset or schema + generate), NOT convert. No PDF is required or requested.

behavioral.text: Claude did NOT try to convert a document or ask for a PDF
Claude produced tabular rows via generate
